REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS LA COSTA LOANS, INC., RICHARD SHRIGLEY, AND SHRIGLEY HOLDINGS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AND FAILS TO STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT EACH CLAIM. IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT PLAINTIFF ARE TO: (1) ALLEGE FACTS TO ESTABLISH STANDING OF EACH PLAINTIFF TO SUE EACH DEFENDANT FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION; (2) ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH CAUSE OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION; IF LIABILITY IS BASED ON CONSPIRACY, TO SO ALLEGE AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION; PLAINTIFF TO MAKE AMENDMENTS IN GOOD FAITH AND IN LIGHT OF AUTHORITIES CITED BY DEFENDANTS AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION (3) TO CLARITY WHETHER PS WERE LENDERS OR INVESTORS; (4) TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT THE RECORDED INSTRUMENTS, INCLUDING POST-FORECLOSURE INSTRUMENTS ARE VOID OR VOIDABLE AND THAT CANCELLATION OF THE INSTRUMENTS WOULD RESULT IN PLAINTIFFS "TITLE, THEIR INTERESTS IN AND POSSESSION OF THE SUBJECT PROPERTIES AND DEFENDANTS' OTHER PROPERTIES." (FAC 57). THE COURT DOES NOT MAKE ANY OTHER RULINGS AT THIS TIME. ALL OTHER ARGUMENTS MAY BE RAISED BY DEFENDANTS AT A LATER TIME, IF NECESSARY AND PROPER, AFTER AMENDS HAVE BEEN MADE. ALL AMENDMENTS TO BE IN BOLD FONT. THIS IS THE FIRST TIME THE COURT LOOKS AT THIS COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501,DEFENDANT SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC, CHONG HONG MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF APPROVED AS TO FORM AN ORDER PROVIDING PLAINTIFF 30 DAYS FROM ENTRY OF ORDER (AS OPPOSED FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY ORDER) TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DO SO. SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS UNTIMELY. THE COURT NOTES THAT IN THE ABSENCE OF THE AGREEMENT BY THE PARTIES, AS IT HAPPENED HERE, THE COURT GENERALLY AUTHORIZES STATUTORILY MANDATED 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. HOWEVER, AS THE DEFENDANT AGREED TO A 30 DAY LEAVE TO AMEND (DEFENDANT PREPARED THE ORDER) AND PLAINTIFF AGREED THAT THE 30 DAYS WOULD RUN FROM THE ENTRY OF ORDER (PLAINTIFF APPROVED THE ORDER AS TO FORM WITHOUT OBJECTIONS), PLAINTIFF HAD 30 DAYS FROM DECEMBER 17, 2018 TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DO SO. IMPROPER REQUEST FOR CCP 473(B) REQUIRES IN OPPOSITION IS NOT CONSIDERED. OTHER AUTHORITIES CITED IN OPPOSITION ARE NOT APPLICABLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC, CHONG HONG DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court has previously sustained demurrers in this action with specific instructions regarding amendments necessary to address arguments raised by the demurrers. Amended pleadings failed to comply with the Court's instructions and no explanation for such failure is provided in the new pleading or in opposition to the demurrer. While the Fourth Amended Complaint is over 20 pages longer than the First Amended Complaint, the factual allegations regarding standing, contract-based and tort-based claims are insufficient. Plaintiff shall have the final opportunity to make factual allegations to support their claims. General conclusory statements are not sufficient. For any breach of contractual provisions the Plaintiff shall attach any written contracts and allege the terms of any oral contracts. For any claims of fraud and concealment factual allegations with requisite particularity (who, when, how, to whom, etc.) shall be alleged. Amendments shall also address November 20, 2018 ruling. All factual allegations and all amendments made pursuant to this ruling shall be in bold font. If factual allegations and amendments are again not made in bold font, Defendant may bring an ex-parte application to strike the non-complaint amended complaint. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC, CHONG HONG MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiff approved as to form an order providing Plaintiff 30 days from entry of order (as opposed from notice of entry order) to file an amended complaint. Plaintiff failed to do so. Second Amended Complaint is untimely. The Court notes that in the absence of the agreement by the parties, as it happened here, the Court generally authorizes statutorily mandated 10 days of notice of entry of order. However, as the Defendant agreed to a 30 day leave to amend (Defendant prepared the order) and Plaintiff agreed that the 30 days would run from the entry of order (Plaintiff approved the order as to form without objections), Plaintiff had 30 days from December 17, 2018 to file an amended complaint without leave of Court. Plaintiff failed to do so. Improper request for CCP 473(b) requires in opposition is not considered. Other authorities cited in opposition are not applicable. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Patrick D'Arcy, esq. (949-988-9640) for Defendants LA COSTA LOANS, INC., RICHARD SHRIGLEY, and SHRIGLEY HOLDINGS, INC. via Court Call.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC and CHONG HONG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs' complaint is uncertain and fails to state facts to support each claim. In the amended complaint Plaintiff are to: (1) allege facts to establish standing of each Plaintiff to sue each defendant for each cause of action; (2) allege facts in support of each cause of each element of each cause of action; if liability is based on conspiracy, to so allege as to each cause of action; Plaintiff to make amendments in good faith and in light of authorities cited by Defendants as to each cause of action (3) to clarity whether Ps were lenders or investors; (4) to allege facts establishing that the recorded instruments, including post-foreclosure instruments are void or voidable and that cancellation of the instruments would result in Plaintiffs "title, their interests in and possession of the Subject Properties and Defendants' Other Properties." (FAC 57). The Court does not make any other rulings at this time. All other arguments may be raised by Defendants at a later time, if necessary and proper, after amends have been made. All amendments to be in bold font. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
